21.correct: Zoji La is the mountain pass that connects Ladakh with the Kashmir Valley (on the Srinagar-Leh route).

wrong.A: Nathu La connects Sikkim with Tibet.
wrong.B: Rohtang Pass is in Himachal Pradesh.
wrong.D: Shipki La is in Himachal Pradesh.

21.fact: Zoji La often closes in winter due to heavy snow; a tunnel is being built to keep it open.

22.correct: Before GST, states introduced Value Added Tax (VAT) mainly to minimise the cascading ('tax on tax') effect of indirect taxation.

wrong.A: VAT replaced sales tax (an indirect tax), not all direct taxes.
wrong.C: It aimed to improve, not increase, fiscal deficits.
wrong.D: It was not designed to raise tariffs for protection.

22.fact: GST (2017) further unified indirect taxes and removed cascading across the country.

23.correct: Hiuen Tsang's (Xuanzang's) accounts are especially valuable for studying the court of Harshavardhana, whom he visited in the 7th century.

wrong.A: He did not describe Harappan towns (a far earlier civilization).
wrong.C: He is not the main source for Mauryan polity (that is Megasthenes/Kautilya).
wrong.D: He did not record Chola naval expeditions.

23.fact: Hiuen Tsang also studied at the Nalanda university during his stay in India.

24.correct: The Fifth Five-Year Plan (1974-79) emphasised poverty removal ('Garibi Hatao') and self-reliance.

wrong.A: Export-led growth was not the Fifth Plan's focus.
wrong.C: Banking reforms were not its theme.
wrong.D: Heavy industry was the focus of the Second Plan.

24.fact: The Fifth Plan was cut short and the Rolling Plan concept was introduced afterwards.

25.correct: Disguised (hidden) unemployment is generally found in the agricultural sector, where more people work on a piece of land than are actually needed.

wrong.B: The IT sector does not typically have disguised unemployment.
wrong.C: The industrial sector is less prone to it.
wrong.D: The service sector is less characterised by disguised unemployment.

25.fact: In disguised unemployment, removing some workers would not reduce total output.

26.correct: The Charter Act of 1813 ended the East India Company's trade monopoly over India, except for the tea trade and trade with China.

wrong.A: It did not specifically end an opium-trade monopoly here.
wrong.C: It was about Indian trade, not just Europe.

26.fact: The 1833 Charter Act later ended even the China-tea monopoly, turning the Company into an administrator.

27.correct: In 2025, the Tripura state government launched the 'Mukhyamantri Balika Samriddhi Yojana'.

wrong.A: Mizoram did not launch this scheme.

27.fact: Such schemes aim to support the education and welfare of the girl child.

28.correct: The Online Gaming Bill (passed by the Lok Sabha in August 2025) promotes e-sports and online social games (I) while banning/regulating online real-money gaming - so 'Only I' is correct.

wrong.B: 'Only II' is wrong; the bill does not promote online money-gaming.
wrong.C: 'Both' is wrong since money-gaming is restricted, not promoted.
wrong.D: 'Neither' is wrong since e-sports are promoted.

28.fact: The bill seeks to curb the harms of online real-money/betting games while supporting legitimate e-sports.

29.correct: 'Matki' is a popular folk dance of Madhya Pradesh, performed by women balancing pots.

wrong.A: It is not from Andhra Pradesh.
wrong.C: It is not from West Bengal.

29.fact: The Matki dance is performed by the Malwa region women on festive occasions.

30.correct: In December 2024, Masali village (in Gujarat's Banaskantha district) was declared India's first border solar village.

wrong.A: Mana (Maana) in Uttarakhand is called India's 'first village', not the first solar border village.
wrong.C: Mawlynnong is known as Asia's cleanest village.
wrong.D: Khonoma is known as India's first green village.

30.fact: The solar village uses rooftop solar to meet its electricity needs.

31.correct: The Pongal festival in Tamil Nadu is celebrated over 4 days (Bhogi, Thai Pongal, Mattu Pongal and Kaanum Pongal).
31.fact: Pongal is a harvest/thanksgiving festival dedicated to the Sun God.

32.correct: As per the 2011 Census, Bihar had the highest population density among Indian states (about 1,102 persons per sq km).

wrong.A: Kerala's density is lower than Bihar's.
wrong.C: Nagaland has a low population density.
wrong.D: Haryana's density is lower than Bihar's.

32.fact: Bihar is among India's most densely populated and populous states.

33.correct: The melting point of ice is 273.15 Kelvin (0 degrees Celsius).

wrong.A: 373.15 K is the boiling point of water.
wrong.D: 100 K is far below the melting point.

33.fact: To convert Celsius to Kelvin, add 273.15.

34.correct: Goa is famous for both manganese ore (I) and iron ore (II), so 'Both I and II' is correct.

wrong.D: 'Neither' is wrong since Goa has both.

34.fact: Iron-ore mining and export have historically been important to Goa's economy.

35.correct: The Pro Kabaddi League was launched in 2014.
35.fact: The Pro Kabaddi League greatly boosted the popularity and professionalism of kabaddi in India.

36.correct: Dhaka hosted the South Asian (Federation) Games in 2010.
36.fact: The South Asian Games are a regional multi-sport event among SAARC nations.

37.correct: The cricket umpire signal shown - crossing and uncrossing the wrists below the waist - indicates a 'Dead Ball'.

wrong.A: A 'leg bye' is signalled by tapping a raised knee.
wrong.B: A 'short run' is signalled by tapping the near shoulder.
wrong.C: A 'four' is signalled by waving the arm across the chest.

37.fact: A dead ball means no runs can be scored and the delivery does not count for certain purposes.

38.correct: The Biodiversity Beyond National Jurisdiction (BBNJ) agreement is an international treaty under the UN Convention on the Law of the Sea (UNCLOS).

wrong.B: The WTO deals with trade, not high-seas biodiversity.
wrong.C: The 'WEO' is not the relevant body.
wrong.D: UNESCO is not the framework for this treaty.

38.fact: The BBNJ ('High Seas Treaty') aims to conserve marine biodiversity in areas beyond national jurisdiction.

39.correct: The International IP Index 2025 is the 13th edition of the index.
39.fact: The International IP Index is released by the US Chamber of Commerce, ranking IP protection across economies.

40.correct: Per the World Bank's Logistics Performance Index 2023, India climbed to 22nd in the 'international shipments' category.

wrong.B: 12th is not India's rank in that category.

40.fact: India's overall LPI 2023 rank improved to 38th, reflecting better logistics infrastructure.
